name: Anisplatzchen
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon anise extract or 3 teaspoons anise seed
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the eggs in a mixer until fluffy and gradually add the sugar, beating continuously.
2. Continue to beat for 20 minutes (this time is correct).
3. Gradually add the sifted dry ingredients and beat for another three minutes.
4. Add the anise extract.
5. Drop spoonfuls of dough onto well-greased and floured cookie sheets, forming round cookies by swirling the dough with a spoon.
6. Allow to stand until slightly firm overnight.
7. Bake at 325°F for about 15 minutes or until the cookies are a light golden color.
8. Yield: About 3 1/2 dozen cookies.
NOTE: Don't even try these unless you like anise. This is an entirely different tasting cookie which originated in Germany. They are very unusual as they are self-frosted on top and can be made well ahead of the holidays and stored in airtight tins.
